disputedBy.claim: Pre-invasion Allied intelligence (Cross-Channel Attack §VII) credited the 716. ID with substantially more strength than Zetterling's archival reconstruction (~8 500 vs ~7 000). The discrepancy turns on how Ost-battalion fillers and divisional artillery are counted ; Zetterling excludes attached non-organic units.

waypoints.note: Static coastal-defence division holding the sector from the Vire estuary to the Orne. Divisional HQ in the Caen suburbs ; centroid drifted west of Caen since the division's strongpoints are spread along ~50 km of coast.
H-Hour : the Gold/Juno/Sword sectors are within 716. ID's frontage. Strongpoints (WN) take naval bombardment and assault landings simultaneously.
Forward strongpoints overrun across all three British/Canadian beaches ; reserves committed piecemeal. The static division can't manoeuvre.
Mid-day: 716. ID losing cohesion ; surviving elements falling back through the Périers ridge / Caen north outskirts toward 21. Pz lines.
Mid-afternoon : centroid pulled south of Caen-Carpiquet ; remaining battalions entangled with 21. Panzer's counterattack assembly area.
End of MVP window: 716. ID effectively combat-broken ; survivors absorbed by 21. Panzer or holding fragmentary positions south of Caen. Position is approximate divisional centroid.
